title: 9 Herbal Tinctures For Chickenpox
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a helpful way to alleviate some of the symptoms of chickenpox. For example, Echinacea purpurea is known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the severity of the rash and fever associated with the illness. It's also believed to boost the immune system, helping your body to fight off the virus more effectively. Calendula officinalis, on the other hand, is often used to soothe and calm irritated skin, which can be a big relief for people with chickenpox. Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, has antihistamine properties that can help to reduce itching and inflammation. Drinking herbal teas made with these herbs can help to bring down your body temperature, reduce the severity of the rash, and even help to prevent further outbreaks. By incorporating these herbs into your treatment plan, you may be able to alleviate some of the discomfort and stress associated with chickenpox.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures contains alkylamides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms by fighting off the underlying viral infection. Alkylamides, in particular, have been shown to stimulate the production of white blood cells, which are vital for fighting off viral infections. Flavonoids and phenolic acids have antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the skin. By taking Echinacea purpurea tinctures, some people have reported a reduction in the duration and severity of their chickenpox symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Echinacea purpurea roots and flowers, and 2 cups of 80-proof vodka.
Chop the Echinacea roots and flowers finely.
Combine the chopped Echinacea with the vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, and store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin. The flavonoids in calendula tinctures also have antiviral properties, which can help to reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms. The antiseptic properties of triterpenoid saponins in calendula tinctures can help to prevent infection and promote wound healing. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, calendula officinalis tinctures can help to alleviate some of the discomfort associated with chickenpox.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers and 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the Calendula flowers and vodka in the jar, making sure the flowers are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to small dropper bottles and store them in a cool, dark place. Use 10-15 drops of the tincture in water or juice 3-4 times a day to help soothe chickenpox symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tinctures contains bioactive constituents like histamine, flavonoids, and saponins. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with chickenpox. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin, also have antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, the saponins in Urtica dioica tinctures have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent secondary infections that can worsen chickenpox symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Urtica dioica tinctures can help alleviate the discomfort and complications associated with chickenpox.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal tinctures for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and flowers, clean them, and chop them finely.
Combine the chopped Urtica dioica with 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar. The jar should be at least 1/2 filled with the herb.
Seal the jar tightly and shake it well for 10 minutes to mix the Urtica dioica and vodka.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place and shake it every day for 2 weeks. This allows the tincture to steep and mature.

After 2 weeks, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. Use 5-10 drops of the tincture in water 3-4 times a day to help manage chickenpox symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures contains hyperforin, a key active constituent, and other flavonoids like quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce redness and swelling associated with chickenpox. Hyperforin also has antiviral properties, which can help combat the herpes zoster virus that causes chickenpox. The flavonoids in Hypericum perforatum tinctures can also help reduce the severity of symptoms such as fever and itching. The antihistamine properties of the tannins present in the tincture can help alleviate itching and skin irritation associated with chickenpox.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal tinctures for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 250g of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Combine the dried flowers with 750ml of vodka in the glass jar.
Close the jar and shake it well for 2 minutes to mix the flowers and vodka.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and store the tincture in the fridge.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale tinctures contains taraxasterol, a triterpene saponin that has anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce redness and swelling associated with chickenpox. Taraxasterol also has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent secondary infections that can make chickenpox worse. The tinctures also contain taraxol, a sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help combat the herpes zoster virus that causes chickenpox. By reducing inflammation and fighting off the virus, Taraxacum officinale tinctures may help alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Taraxacum officinale herbal tinctures for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Taraxacum officinale flowers and 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar with the flowers, making sure to leave 1 inch of space at the top.
Add the vodka to the jar, making sure the flowers are fully covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tinctures contains rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound. This compound helps reduce redness and swelling associated with chickenpox, making it easier to manage symptoms. Rosmarinic acid also has antiviral properties, which can help combat the virus that causes chickenpox. Additionally, Melissa officinalis contains apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and protect the skin. By reducing inflammation and fighting the virus, Melissa officinalis tinctures can help alleviate the discomfort and severity of chickenpox symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal tinctures for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis leaves, 2 cups of 80% vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the Melissa officinalis leaves and vodka in the glass jar, making sure the leaves are covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in the fridge and take 10-20 drops, 3-4 times a day, for relief from chickenpox symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tinctures contains bioactive constituents like sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and volatile oils. These compounds help reduce inflammation and fever, which are common symptoms of chickenpox. The tannins in Achillea millefolium have antiviral properties, which can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox. The tincture's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties also aid in preventing secondary infections, making it a useful natural remedy for managing chickenpox symptoms. By reducing fever, inflammation, and the risk of infection, Achillea millefolium tinctures can help alleviate the discomfort associated with chickenpox.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal tinctures for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers. Clean and dry them.
Combine 1 cup of the dried plant material with 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place. Use 2-3 drops per 1 cup of water, 3-4 times a day, for chickenpox symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures contains the bioactive constituents aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with chickenpox. The tannins and anthraquinones in aloe barbadensis also have antiviral properties, which may help combat the herpes zoster virus that causes chickenpox. Additionally, the gel-like substance in aloe barbadensis has soothing and protective properties, which can help calm the skin and reduce the risk of infection. By applying aloe barbadensis tinctures to the affected areas, the skin may be able to heal faster and with less scarring.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal tinctures for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis leaves, 2 cups of vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Wash the Aloe leaves with water, then chop them into small pieces.
Place the chopped Aloe in the glass jar and pour the vodka over it. Make sure the leaves are completely covered.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon tinctures contains salicylic acid, a key bioactive constituent that helps to reduce inflammation and relieve itching associated with chickenpox. The tannins in Vaccinium macrocarpon have astringent properties, which can help to dry out blisters and reduce the risk of infection. Additionally, Vaccinium macrocarpon contains anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that can help to soothe and calm the skin. The anti-inflammatory properties of Vaccinium macrocarpon may also help to reduce the severity and duration of chickenpox symptoms. By reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, Vaccinium macrocarpon tinctures can help to alleviate some of the discomfort associated with chickenpox.
remedies.image_alt: Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal tinctures for chickenpox
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Vaccinium macrocarpon berries, 2 cups of 80% vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the Vaccinium macrocarpon berries and vodka in the glass jar. Make sure the berries are completely covered by the vodka.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in the refrigerator and use 5-10 drops per 1 cup of water as needed for chickenpox symptoms.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea can't prevent chickenpox from forming. Chickenpox is a viral infection caused by the varicella-zoster virus, and herbal tea doesn't have the power to stop its spread. Some herbal teas, like peppermint or chamomile, may help soothe symptoms like fever and itching, but they won't prevent the illness.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for chickenpox daily is not recommended. Some herbal teas can interfere with the body's natural healing process. They may slow down the recovery time or even make symptoms worse. It's best to stick to drinking plenty of water and clear fluids to help your body fight off the infection.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with chickenpox symptoms, but it's hard to predict exactly when you'll feel better. It usually takes 2-5 days for the rash to start fading, and 1-2 weeks for the blisters to fully heal. Some people may notice improvement in a few days, while others may take longer.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea when you have chickenpox can help with discomfort. It's best to have it in the evening, as it can help you relax and calm your skin. This can make it easier to fall asleep and feel better when you wake up. Many people find it soothing to drink a warm cup before bed.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for chickenpox. For example, valerian root tea can slow down liver function, which could affect how well your body absorbs antiviral medicines. Licorice root tea can also affect liver function and may interact with certain medications. Be cautious when drinking herbal teas with prescription chickenpox treatments.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have chickenpox, it's essential to be cautious about what you drink. Herbal teas might seem harmless, but some can affect your baby. Certain herbs, like pennyroyal and sage, can cause problems. It's best to stick with plain water or decaf tea.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding and dealing with chickenpox, it's best to avoid herbal teas. Some teas may contain ingredients that can pass into breast milk and affect your baby. If you're concerned about your baby's health, consider sticking to plain water or milk instead.
